action.reasoning: Observation: Robot faces away from human (angle≈±π) and oscillates between +π and -π, never completing the turn. The angular velocity flips sign each step. Analysis: When angle is near ±π boundary, small perturbations cause sign flips. Need to commit to one turn direction when |angle| > π/2. Also the robot goes backward when it should be turning in place or moving forward. Decision: Fix the controller to handle wrap-around properly - always turn in the shorter direction and commit to it. Also always move forward slightly while turning.
